mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Persistent inflation above 3.5% (CPIAUCSL) triggers central bank tightening expectations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as risk assets face higher discount rates.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 60% of instances where CPIAUCSL exceeds 3.5%, or the signal's IC drops below -0.05 in rolling 1-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in payroll employment (PAYEMS) and declining unemployment (UNRATE) signals strong labor market dynamics that boost corporate earnings visibility, generating positive S&P 500 returns over 60 days.
failure_condition: The interaction term between PAYEMS and UNRATE fails to explain > 15% of SPX return variance in 3-year rolling windows, or returns are negative in 60%+ of triggering instances.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO) combined with declining housing starts (HOUST) indicates supply-side bottlenecks that drive inflationary pressures, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: The combined INDPRO-HOUST signal explains < 10% of SPX return variation in 2-year rolling windows, or returns are positive in 60%+ of triggering instances.

mechanism_narrative: A rebound in consumer spending (RSAFS) following a recessionary dip predicts a V-shaped recovery, generating positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as market participants price in economic revival.
failure_condition: SPX fails to produce positive 20d returns in 60%+ of instances where RSAFS shows YoY growth above 5%, or the signal's IC drops below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Weak housing starts (HOUST) during a monetary tightening cycle signals reduced consumer confidence, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days as risk appetite contracts.
failure_condition: The HOUST-FEDFUNDS interaction fails to explain > 12% of SPX return variance in 2-year rolling windows, or returns are positive in 50%+ of triggering instances.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in unemployment (UNRATE) without corresponding wage growth predicts a labor market overheating scenario, causing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 days as inflation fears dominate market sentiment.
failure_condition: The UNRATE-CPIAUCSL interaction explains < 8% of SPX return variance in 3-year rolling windows, or returns are positive in 55%+ of triggering instances.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent declines in industrial production (INDPRO) indicate weakening supply-side capacity, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as recessionary risks materialize.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 50% of instances where INDPRO falls below 100, or the signal's IC drops below -0.04.
